description_long: This sculpture is a large, monolithic stone head with pronounced facial features, including a broad nose, deep-set eyes, and full lips. The surface is rough and pitted, with a mottled coloration of pinkish and grayish tones, suggesting weathered stone. The head is rectangular and blocky, with stylized ears protruding from the sides and a prominent ridge running from the top of the forehead to the back. The base is a simple rectangular plinth, also made of stone, with what appears to be an inscription or plaque on the front. The overall form is reminiscent of ancient monumental heads, possibly inspired by Mesoamerican Olmec sculptures. The sculpture exudes a sense of weight and permanence, suitable for display in a public or outdoor urban setting.
description: A large, weathered stone head sculpture with pronounced facial features and a rectangular base, reminiscent of ancient monumental art. The surface is rough and mottled with pink and gray tones.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full face with deep-set eyes, broad nose, and lips, as well as the inscription or plaque on the base.
description_view_1: The left-side view highlights the protruding ear and the pronounced ridge running over the head, with the facial features partially visible.
description_view_2: The rear-left view shows the back of the head, which is less detailed and more block-like, with the ear and ridge still visible.
description_view_3: The right-side view displays the right ear and the side profile of the face, with the ridge and blocky form evident.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a neutral background.
